title: Cost-Benefit Evaluation
concept: Calculate cost-per-thousand-tokens: evaluate whether the accuracy boost of a large model justifies its higher API/hosting expenses.

exercises.prompt: If you want to build an AI that simply capitalizes the first letter of every sentence, which model should you choose?
exercises.explain: If a task is extremely simple, even a basic, low-cost model will achieve near-perfect accuracy, making expensive models a waste of money.

cards.title: The Million-Dollar Question
cards.body: Imagine launching a cool new app that goes viral overnight! Millions of users are chatting with your AI. But at the end of the month, you get a server bill for $50,000. How do we build smart AI features without going broke? Let's learn how to balance smarts with cents!

cards.title: What is Cost-Benefit Evaluation?
cards.body: Cost-Benefit Evaluation in AI means calculating the cost-per-thousand-tokens (or per million tokens) of different models and comparing it against their accuracy. It helps you decide if paying 10x more for a smarter model actually brings enough business value to justify the massive price jump.

cards.title: The Delivery Vehicle Dilemma
cards.body: Think of choosing an AI model like picking a delivery vehicle. A massive, heavy-duty cargo truck (like a huge frontier model) can carry highly fragile, complex cargo perfectly, but it costs a fortune in gas. A small scooter (like a tiny local model) is incredibly cheap and fast for delivering a single envelope, but it can't handle a grand piano. You only pay for the heavy truck when the cargo's value justifies the fuel cost.

cards.title: Chatbot Budgeting in Action
cards.body:
Imagine a customer service chatbot. 
- Model Fast-AI costs $0.50 per million tokens and gets 85% of answers right.
- Model Ultra-AI costs $15.00 per million tokens (30x more!) and gets 95% of answers right.
For basic FAQs, Fast-AI is the winner because paying 30 times more just to get 10% more accuracy is not worth the budget drain.

cards.title: Myth: Smarter is Always Better
cards.body: It's easy to assume that the smartest model is always the best choice for your app. In reality, using a giant, expensive model for simple tasks like summarizing short text or sorting support tickets is like using a spaceship to drive to the grocery store. Simple models save thousands of dollars!

cards.title: The Trade-off Comparison
cards.body: When selecting a model, compare the price multiplier against the accuracy boost. Often, a small drop in accuracy saves a massive amount of money.

exercises.prompt: Why do developers calculate the cost-per-thousand-tokens when choosing an AI model?

options.text: To measure how fast the model responds to the user.
To estimate the ongoing API hosting costs based on text volume.
To find out how many parameters the model has in its brain.
To see if the model can generate high-quality images.

exercises.explain: The cost-per-token tells you exactly how much you are billed for the amount of text processed, allowing you to estimate your monthly expenses based on usage volume.

exercises.prompt: True or False: If Model A is 10 times cheaper than Model B, you should always choose Model A.
exercises.explain: A model that is 10 times cheaper only makes financial sense if the drop in accuracy is acceptable for your specific application. If you are doing medical diagnoses, even a 1% drop could be unacceptable.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the rule of thumb for AI budgeting:
exercises.sentence: To choose the right model, you must evaluate the cost-to-performance ratio.
exercises.explain: Calculating the cost-to-performance ratio helps you determine if the extra accuracy of a premium model is worth the additional price per token.

exercises.prompt: Which of these applications is the best candidate for paying premium prices for a high-accuracy, expensive model?

options.text: An automated system that tags incoming emails as "Spam" or "Inbox".
An AI coding assistant that automatically fixes complex security bugs in software code.
A chatbot that converts user queries into simple search engine keywords.

exercises.explain: AI-generated code fixes require absolute precision to avoid introducing bugs, which justifies the high cost of a flagship model. Classification and sorting are simple tasks where cheaper models perform excellently.

exercises.prompt: You are building a spam filter that processes millions of emails. Model A costs $10.00 per million tokens and has 90% accuracy. Model B costs $1.00 per million tokens and has 80% accuracy. Which model is the more cost-effective choice for a basic spam folder?

options.text: Model A, because 90% accuracy is always required regardless of the cost.
Model B, because it costs 10x less while only losing 10% in accuracy.
Model A, because larger models are always more cost-effective in the long run.
Model B, but only if we run it on local hardware instead of an API.

exercises.explain: To find the most cost-effective choice, we compare the cost per 1,000 successful classifications. For Model A, 1,000 runs cost $10.00 and yield 900 correct answers ($0.011 per correct answer). For Model B, 1,000 runs cost $1.00 and yield 800 correct answers ($0.00125 per correct answer). Model B is vastly cheaper for nearly the same utility!
